input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android 15+ / API 35), edge-to-edge display became the default, and `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30. The recommended approach now is to use the `KeyboardAcceleratorsChanged` event or handle insets properly.


The modern solution involves using Android's `WindowInsetsCompat` API to listen for keyboard insets and adjust the layout padding dynamically, since the old `adjustResize` approach no longer works reliably with edge-to-edge layouts. I can outline a few practical approaches, including using the manifest setting as a fallback, but the inset-based approach is the most robust for .NET 10.
